## Overview

Episodic memory is the system for personal experiences — events tagged with their original context of time, place, and self. Distinct from semantic memory (facts) and procedural memory (skills), episodic memory lets you mentally re-experience past events.

The cognitive architecture distinguishes episodic memory from semantic memory: episodic stores experience, semantic stores generalized knowledge.
